zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Agnes Maitland於1385年出生在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安克萊頓時，她的父親Robert Maitland16歲，母親Marian Abernethy14歲。她於1413年之前與William John de Erskine結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1421年在她的家鄉享年36歲。

nameMeanings.name: Maitland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): nickname for an ungracious individual, from Old French maltalant, mautalent ‘anger, vexation, spite’ (from Late Latin malum ‘bad’ + talentum ‘inclination, disposition’).

variants.name: Maitlen
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of Scottish andEnglish   Maitland  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Agnes Maitland was born in 1385, in Crichton, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Maitland, was 16 and her mother, Marian Abernethy, was 14. She married William John de Erskine before 1413. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in 1421, in her hometown, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Agnes Maitland est née en 1385, à Crichton, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Maitland, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Marian Abernethy, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé William John d’Erskine avant 1413. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1421, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Agnes Maitland 1385 in Crichton, Midlothian, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Maitland 16 und ihre Mutter Marian Abernethy 14. Sie hat vor 1413 William John de Erskine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1421 im Alter von 36 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Agnes Maitland è nata il nel 1385 nel Crichton, Midlothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert Maitland, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Marian Abernethy, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato William John de Erskine prima del 1413. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel 1421, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 36 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Agnes Maitland(이)가 1385년 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 크리튼에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Maitland(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marian Abernethy(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1413년 이전 William John de Erskine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1421년 그녀의 고향에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Agnes Maitland nasceu em 1385, em Crichton, Midlothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Maitland, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Marian Abernethy, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com William John de Erskine antes 1413. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1421, em sua cidade natal, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Agnes Maitland родилась в 1385 г. в местности кричтон, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert Maitland было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Marian Abernethy, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William John de Erskine перед 1413 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1421 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 36 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Agnes Maitland nació en 1385, en Crichton, Midlothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Maitland, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Marian Abernethy, tenía 14 años. Se casó con William John de Erskine antes de 1413. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió en 1421, en su pueblo, a la edad de 36 años.
